publications.title: Public T cell clonotypes are selected in HLA-B∗57:01+/HIV+ patients independently of the viral load.
publications.authors: Chatzileontiadou DSM, Lobos CA, Robson H, Almedia CA, Szeto C, Castley A, D'Orsogna LJ, Gras S.
publications.executive_summary: This study examines TW10-specific T cell responses in HLA-B∗57:01–positive HIV+ patients, focusing on the Gag-derived epitope TW10 presented by the protective HLA-B∗57:01 allele. The researchers identified a striking public TCR clonotype and strong TRBV5 bias in the TW10-specific TCR repertoire, and solved the crystal structure of the HLA-B∗57:01–TW10–TCR complex to reveal the structural basis for this biased recognition. Despite low TCR affinity, TW10-specific T cells mounted polyfunctional responses independently of viral load, suggesting that TCR-pMHC geometry and public clonotype selection—rather than affinity or antigen abundance—drive protective T cell control in HIV controllers. This work provides mechanistic insight into why a particular HLA allele and epitope are enriched in HIV controllers and has implications for understanding TCR repertoire constraints in infection-driven T cell selection and vaccine design targeting conserved viral epitopes.
